_meta.title: Muscular System
sources: Tom Myers — Anatomy Trains (fascial lines reference)

system.name: Muscular System
system.shortDescription: ~640 skeletal muscles producing movement, posture, and heat. Plus smooth muscle (organs) and cardiac muscle. The body's primary force-generating and locomotor tissue.

primaryStructures: Smooth muscle (involuntary — organ walls, blood vessels, GI tract)
Fascia (connective tissue investing muscle — Tom Myers' Anatomy Trains)

shoulder_girdle: Rotator cuff (supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, subscapularis)

anatomy.vertebralCorrelates: Spinal nerves innervate all skeletal muscle. Myotomes (motor) and dermatomes (sensory) map root-by-root. Each muscle has a specific innervation pattern.
anatomy.nervePlexus: Brachial plexus (upper limb), lumbosacral plexus (lower limb), cervical plexus (neck/diaphragm via phrenic). Spinal motor anterior horn cells.

astrologicalRulership.primaryPlanetRationale: Mars = action, movement, force generation. Cornell (1933): 'Mars rules the muscular system, the motor function, the active contraction.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: All skeletal muscle activation, motor drive, force generation
Chronic muscle tension/tightness, structural musculo-skeletal restriction, age-related sarcopenia
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn restricts muscular freedom; chronic stiffness.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Vital force feeding muscle, cardiac muscle (heart), core muscular vitality
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell (1933): 'The Sun in Leo rules the muscles of the heart.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Smooth muscle (involuntary — gut, vessels), uterine muscle, breast tissue
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Moon rules involuntary functions, smooth muscle.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Muscle bulk (when balanced), hip/thigh musculature, expansive muscular growth
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Jupiter expands; muscular development in Sagittarius (hips/thighs).'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Fine motor coordination, muscle proprioception, neuromuscular signaling
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Mercury rules the nerves of muscle action.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Muscle softness/relaxation, lower-back/lumbar musculature, throat muscles
Sudden muscle spasm, cramping, twitches, exercise-induced rhabdomyolysis
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Modern: Uranus governs sudden electrical-muscular events.

astrologicalRulership.fixedCrossInfluence: Less central for muscular than for cardiac. However, Scorpio (deep tissue) affects deep psoas/pelvic floor; Taurus (neck/throat muscles); Leo (cardiac muscle); Aquarius (calves, peripheral muscle).
secondarySigns: Each sign rules specific muscle groups per medical astrology body-map tradition
Sagittarius (hips, thighs — gluteals, hip flexors, quadriceps, hamstrings)

axes.name: Renal Axis (lower back muscular)
axes.interpretation: Aries (head, neck activation) opposite Libra (lower back, kidneys). Lumbar QL + psoas tension often tracks this axis. Adrenal (Aries) overdrive depletes the structural support (Libra).

axes.name: Cardio-Circulatory Axis (cardiac + skeletal muscle)
axes.interpretation: Leo (heart muscle, thoracic spine) opposite Aquarius (calves, peripheral circulation to muscle). Cardiac muscle is part of muscular system.

energetics.chakra: Root (Muladhara) for grounding + structural; Solar Plexus (Manipura) for action/will
energetics.nervousSystemPathway: Spinal motor → anterior horn cell → peripheral motor nerve → neuromuscular junction → muscle fiber
energetics.endocrineCorrespondence: Adrenal medulla (acute Mars), adrenal cortex (cortisol affects muscle breakdown), testes (anabolic), pancreas (insulin for muscle glucose uptake)
energetics.brainwaveAffinity: Beta during action; Alpha during recovery
energetics.meridianCrossover_TCM: Spleen meridian (governs muscle tissue per TCM). Liver meridian (tendons). Bladder meridian (back muscles).

cellSalts.indicatedFor: Muscle cramps, spasms, charley horses, menstrual cramps, restless legs. Take in HOT water for fastest action.
Acute muscle inflammation, strain/sprain (first 24-48hr), DOMS reduction, soft tissue trauma.
Tendon weakness, ligament laxity, post-injury elastic tissue rebuild, varicose veins.
Tendon insertion problems, slow muscle recovery from injury, growing pains in children.
Post-exercise nervous exhaustion, weakness from depleted nerve fuel.

supplementaryHerbs.name: Cramp bark (Viburnum opulus)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Menstrual cramps, muscle spasm, restless legs

supplementaryHerbs.name: Turmeric (Curcuma longa)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Muscle inflammation, exercise recovery, chronic muscle pain

supplementaryHerbs.name: Arnica (Arnica montana)
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: Topical: reduces bruising, swelling (homeopathic preparations only — toxic if ingested concentrated)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Acute strain/sprain, bruising — TOPICAL ONLY (or homeopathic)

tuningForks.applicationPoint: Directly on muscle belly of involved area

scents.name: Wintergreen (caution)
scents.delivery: topical diluted only — contains methyl salicylate, never internal

modalityRecommendations.individual: Daily movement is non-negotiable for muscular health. Strength training 2-3x/week, mobility/stretching daily, hydration, protein adequate (~0.8-1.2g/kg bodyweight), magnesium (cramp prevention), sleep for muscle repair. Address chronic tension areas (neck, low back) with regular self-care or professional bodywork.
modalityRecommendations.medical_astrologer: Mars + Aries primary. Mars-Saturn for chronic stiffness/tendinopathy. Mars-Uranus for sudden injuries/cramps. Each sign rules specific muscle groups (Aries head/neck, Taurus throat/shoulders, Gemini arms/hands, Cancer chest/breast, Leo back/heart, Virgo abdomen, Libra lower back/kidneys, Scorpio pelvic floor/deep, Sagittarius hips/thighs, Capricorn knees, Aquarius calves/ankles, Pisces feet).
modalityRecommendations.reiki: Mars-active areas: cooling visualization. Chronically tense: red-to-orange-to-green warming. Hand position over involved muscle.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_general: PRIMARY MODALITY for muscular system. Trigger point release (Travell-Simons). Myofascial release. Neuromuscular technique (NMT). Sports massage. Active release technique (ART). Fascial line work (Anatomy Trains framework). Specific to muscle: identify, palpate, release. Combine with mobilization and movement re-education.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_contraindications: Acute strain/sprain (first 48-72hr): RICE protocol, no deep work over injury. DVT suspected: never massage. Anticoagulant therapy: bruising precautions. Recent surgery: clearance required.
modalityRecommendations.naturopathy: Magnesium glycinate or malate (cramping). Turmeric/curcumin (inflammation). Omega-3 (anti-inflammatory). Tart cherry (DOMS). Protein adequacy. Vitamin D (muscle function). Address underlying inflammatory drivers.
modalityRecommendations.acupuncture: Local + distal points for muscle pain. Trigger point dry needling. Bladder meridian (UB 23, 25 for back). Liver meridian (LV 3 for tendon/qi).
modalityRecommendations.ayurveda_phase2_placeholder: Mamsa Dhatu (muscle tissue). Vata excess = muscle tremor/cramping. Pitta excess = inflammation. Kapha excess = muscle heaviness. Abhyanga (oil massage) primary. Phase 2.
modalityRecommendations.pastoral_spiritual: Body as instrument. Movement as prayer. Yoga, tai chi, qigong as embodied practice.

pathologies.name: Muscle Strain / Sprain (Acute)

planetaryCombinations: Mars transit (current — high injury risk window)
transitTriggers: Active Mars transit, especially through fire signs

pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Mars in stress → strain, sprain, sudden injury; Mars-Uranus → unexpected accident.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Acute muscle strain responds to RICE (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation) for 48-72 hours, then gradual mobilization. Persistent pain > 1 week or inability to bear weight warrants medical evaluation.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Active Mars transits classically associated with increased injury risk. Acute strain: 48-72hr RICE, then progressive return. Differentiate strain (muscle) from sprain (ligament) from tear (partial vs complete). Refer for imaging if severe pain, inability to bear weight, or no improvement in 7-10 days.

modalityNotes.bodywork: First 48-72hr: NO deep work over injury. Lymphatic drainage proximal to injury OK. Recovery phase: progressive bodywork, scar tissue prevention.
modalityNotes.reiki: Excellent for acute — distance or above-body work doesn't require physical contact. Cooling intention.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: RICE first 48-72hr. Arnica gel topical. Bromelain (pineapple enzyme) for swelling. Ginger anti-inflammatory.

redFlags: Inability to bear weight or move joint → orthopedic evaluation
Suspected tendon rupture (snap heard, immediate severe weakness) → urgent
Compartment syndrome (severe pain disproportionate to injury, tense compartment) → ED

pathologies.name: Fibromyalgia
pathologies.classicalReference: Modern syndrome. Neptune-Mars patterns align with diffuse central sensitization (modern attribution).

indicatorLanguage.individual: Widespread muscular pain, fatigue, sleep difficulties, and cognitive fog characterize this condition. Multidisciplinary care — gentle movement, sleep optimization, stress management, nervous-system regulation — produces the best outcomes.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Neptune-Mars and Saturn-Moon configurations align with central sensitization patterns (modern attribution). Fibromyalgia is a real but poorly understood syndrome. Approach: gentle movement (graded), sleep hygiene critical, nervous system regulation (vagal practices, restorative bodywork), magnesium, low-dose naltrexone (rheumatology), CBT. Refer for rheumatology consultation to exclude other diagnoses.

modalityNotes.bodywork: GENTLE only. Aggressive work worsens symptoms. Lymphatic drainage, light effleurage, craniosacral, gentle myofascial release. Trauma-informed approach.
modalityNotes.reiki: Excellent — non-touching distance reiki often preferred. Full chakra balancing.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Sleep optimization #1. Graded movement (walking, water exercise, gentle yoga). Magnesium, D, B12, CoQ10. Stress management. Nervous system practices. Address comorbid depression/anxiety.

redFlags: New severe focal weakness → rule out other diagnoses

pathologies.name: Chronic Low Back Pain
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Lumbago — Saturn in Libra; chronic restriction of lumbar function.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Low back pain is extremely common. Most resolves with movement (not bed rest), targeted exercises (especially hip + core strengthening), and addressing posture. Persistent pain > 6 weeks, severe radiating pain, or any bowel/bladder dysfunction with back pain requires medical evaluation.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn in Libra classically associated with chronic lumbar restriction (Cornell, 1933). Differential: muscular (myofascial), discogenic, facet joint, sacroiliac, hip referred. Address: core stability (transverse abdominis, multifidus), hip mobility (psoas, glutes), QL release, fascial work. Refer if red flags.

modalityNotes.bodywork: QL release, psoas release, glute work, thoracolumbar mobilization. Address compensatory patterns (often shoulder/neck involvement). Fascial work along Superficial Back Line.
modalityNotes.reiki: Root + sacral chakras. Red-orange light at lumbar.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Movement (not bed rest). Strengthening (McGill big 3: curl-up, side plank, bird dog). Hip mobility. Anti-inflammatory diet. Address ergonomics.

redFlags: Bowel/bladder dysfunction with back pain → cauda equina, ED
Fever + back pain → rule out infection
History of cancer + new back pain → rule out metastasis

pathologies.name: Tendinopathy (Tendinitis / Tendinosis)

planetaryCombinations: Mars in air signs afflicted (repetitive use)

pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn in muscular signs → chronic tendinous restriction.' Modern: tendinosis is degenerative, not inflammatory — a different care approach than tendinitis.

indicatorLanguage.individual: Tendon pain (often with use, may improve with warming up) responds to: relative rest from aggravating activity, gentle progressive loading (NOT total rest — tendons need load to heal), eccentric exercises (gold standard), and addressing biomechanical factors.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-Mars combinations classically associated with chronic tendinous restriction. Modern: differentiate tendinitis (acute inflammatory) from tendinosis (chronic degenerative). Tendinosis care: eccentric loading, progressive return, address kinetic chain issues. PRP/prolotherapy for refractory cases. Avoid prolonged NSAIDs (impairs tendon healing).

modalityNotes.bodywork: Cross-fiber friction (controversial — some evidence for chronic). Eccentric exercise programming. Address upstream/downstream kinetic chain. Cupping for fascial mobility.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Progressive loading (eccentric primary). Address contributing factors (form, ergonomics, equipment). Collagen + vitamin C (tendon synthesis). Patience — tendons heal slowly (12+ weeks often).

redFlags: Sudden snap with immediate weakness (suspected rupture) → urgent orthopedics

vedicOverlay.ayurvedicTreatments_phase2: Abhyanga (oil massage) primary modality. Mahanarayana taila. Phase 2.

bodywork.outputDepth: Full muscle-resolution map, trigger point reference, fascial line guide

complianceNotes.scopeOfPractice: Muscular complaints are bodyworker's primary scope. Refer for: suspected fracture, tendon rupture, infection, neurological deficit, cancer history with new pain.
criticalReferralRules: Bowel/bladder dysfunction + back pain → ED (cauda equina)
Sudden severe weakness or sensory loss → ED (rule out stroke/cord lesion)
Severe pain disproportionate to injury → rule out compartment syndrome
Fever + spine pain → rule out infection
History of cancer + new bone/muscle pain → rule out metastasis
